questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A unit vector in $x y-$ plane which makes an angle of $45^{\circ}$ with the vector $\vec{i}+\vec{j}$ and an angle of $60^{\circ}$ with the vector $\overrightarrow{3 i}-\overrightarrow{4 j}$ is
questions.solution:
Let $\vec{r}=a \vec{i}+b \vec{j} \Rightarrow a^2+b^2=1 \quad$ ....(1)
Also,
$\cos 45^{\circ}=\frac{\vec{r} \cdot(\vec{i}+\vec{j})}{|\vec{r}||\vec{i}+\vec{j}|} \Rightarrow a+b=1 \quad$....(2)
$\cos 60^{\circ}=\frac{\vec{r} \cdot(3 \vec{i}-4 \vec{j})}{|\vec{r}||3 \vec{i}-4 \vec{j}|} \Rightarrow 3 a-4 b=\frac{5}{2} \quad$ ....(3)
There exists no real values of $a$ and $b$ satisfying (1), (2) and (3) Hence no such unit vector exists
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
